artifacts.content:
Hi Tío Luis,

Mom said I should email you myself instead of just waving on video calls. Also I wanted to say I’m glad you’re okay now after that week you spent in the hospital in San Antonio last summer with COVID. When I went for my Pfizer booster on 1/21/22 at the Austin Community Vaccination Center, the nurse asked if anyone in my family had gotten really sick, and Mom told her about you being in the hospital for a week. They even wrote it in my intake notes, which felt kind of weird because it’s your thing, not mine, but I guess it’s important family history.

I’ve been lucky. My case back in March 2021 was mild compared to that. I tested positive on 3/26 after that exposure on my club team in Austin, at Riverside Community Health Clinic. Dad turned into full-on data nerd and kept this Google Doc with all my pulse oximeter readings (they were always 97–99%) and my temps and stuff. He emailed updates to my pediatrician, Dr. Laura Chen at Barton Creek Pediatrics, and even my homeroom teacher so they knew why I was missing Zoom classes. I mostly remember being tired and annoyed that I couldn’t taste Hot Cheetos.

Now everything’s mostly back to normal, just school and a ton of soccer. We moved to Tucson after Dad got his job here, so I’m playing right wing for Tucson Thunder and trying to get ready to try out for varsity when I start high school. Practices at Arthur Pack are brutal when it’s hot, but at least the winters are nice.

How are you feeling these days? Are you back to full shifts? Mom said you still get tired sometimes. Text me pics of your team’s jerseys again, they were sick.

Love,
Ethan

Hi Grandma Rosa,

Thanks for the caldo last Sunday. It was perfect after our game, even if I burned my tongue because I didn’t wait.

I was thinking about when I had COVID back in March 2021 and how you helped us, and I wanted to say thanks again. I know it was a while ago, but it was a big deal for us. After I tested positive on 3/26 in Austin, Mom and Dad basically turned the house into a quarantine zone. I was stuck in my room 24/7 and poor Sofía had to sleep on a mattress in the living room for like two weeks so I wouldn’t breathe on her. She still jokes that I “stole” her bed.

Mom told me you even canceled your in‑home caregiving shifts for those same two weeks because you were scared of bringing anything back to us after my positive test. That was a lot to give up, and I know your clients rely on you. I remember you leaving food on the porch and waving through the window like some movie.

Dad was walking around with the little pulse oximeter he owns, checking me all the time and typing numbers into that Google Doc he shared with Dr. Chen. He said my oxygen was always between 97 and 99 percent, so it stayed boring, which was good.

Now things feel way more normal here in Tucson. I’m busy with Westview and Tucson Thunder, and we still do Sunday visits when we can. I like when you come over with tamales and we all cram around the table at 4827 W Calle Loma. It makes this house feel less new.

Love you,
Ethan

4/5/2021 – Telehealth follow-up with Dr. Chen (Barton Creek Pediatrics – Austin, TX)

Just finished the video visit sitting on my bed. Dr. Laura Chen said my COVID case really was mild. She asked again about chest pain, palpitations, dizziness, fainting, shortness of breath, all that, and I still haven’t had any of it. She said she was documenting in my chart that there’s no chest pain, no palpitations, no fainting.

She cleared me for a gradual 7–10 day return to soccer as long as I stay symptom-free: start with light jogging and stretching, then non-contact drills, then full scrimmages. Dad liked that she gave an actual plan. He told her he’s been tracking my pulse ox (97–99% every day) and temps in the Google Doc since my positive PCR on 3/26 from Riverside Community Health Clinic. He emails it to her so she can see the trends.

Dad also brought up Grandpa Miguel in Phoenix again and his heart problems. You could tell he’s still anxious about long COVID because of that. Dr. Chen said she understands why he’s worried but that Grandpa’s heart issues in Phoenix aren’t directly related to my infection. She doesn’t think I need any special heart or lung tests right now since I was never hospitalized and my breathing stayed normal.

Goal: be back to full-speed sprints by early May. I want to be totally ready for tournaments and for when we move to Tucson later this year so I can make a good impression on my new club coach.

Remember: listen to my body, stop if any chest pain or weird shortness of breath shows up, and tell Mom/Dad right away.

Event: Pfizer-BioNTech COVID Booster – Ethan
Date: Friday, January 21, 2022
Time: 3:30–4:30 p.m.
Location: Austin Community Vaccination Center (Travis County Health)
Attendees: Ethan, Mom (Marisol)

Notes:

– Third shot (booster) after original Pfizer doses on 6/18/21 and 7/9/21.
– Bring vaccination card and ID.
– Ask nurse if it’s okay to keep full soccer schedule this weekend if I only get mild side effects like last time (sore arm, tired, maybe a small headache).

What actually happened (added later):

The nurse did the usual intake questions. When she asked if anyone in our family had gotten really sick from COVID, Mom told her about Tío Luis in San Antonio being hospitalized for a week last summer. The nurse wrote that in my intake notes even though it wasn’t about my own case, just “family history.” It felt kind of personal but I guess it matters.

She also asked about my own COVID infection back in March 2021. I told her I tested positive on 3/26 after a team exposure in Austin and that my pediatrician, Dr. Chen, did a telehealth follow-up on 4/5/21 and cleared me for a 7–10 day gradual return to soccer because I never had chest pain or breathing problems. I mentioned Dad had tracked my oxygen at home (97–99% on his pulse oximeter) and that I’ve been back in full sports since early May 2021 with no issues.

After the shot, my arm got sore and I felt a little tired that night but still made it to practice the next day.

metadata.title: Pfizer Booster – Austin Community Vaccination Center

artifacts.content:
Group chat: “Thunder Grind” (Ethan, Jace, Malik)

2023-03-08, 9:17 p.m.

Ethan: u guys ever think about how much covid messed stuff up?

Jace: random lol. why?

Ethan: idk, sofia was complaining she had to sleep on the couch last night cuz mom’s cousin is visiting, and I remembered when she had to sleep on a mattress in the living room for 2 weeks while I was locked in my room with covid back in March 2021.

Ethan: like after my positive test on 3/26 in Austin, my mom went full quarantine mode. I wasn’t allowed out of my bedroom and she kept wiping everything. grandma Rosa even canceled her caregiving shifts for those two weeks cuz she was scared of bringing anything back to us.

Malik: dang dude

Ethan: yeah and my dad was walking around with his little pulse oximeter all day, checking me and typing the numbers into this Google Doc. he said my oxygen was always 97–99% so I was “boringly healthy.” he emailed it to my pediatrician, Dr. Chen, like every day lol.

Jace: at least u had a doctor actually talking to you. my parents just gave me gatorade and said “you’re fine”

Ethan: true. dr chen did a video call on 4/5 and gave me this 7–10 day return-to-soccer plan. dad was freaking out cuz my grandpa Miguel in Phoenix has heart problems and he thought I’d get long covid or something.

Malik: and now u run like 10 miles a day at Arthur Pack so guess it worked out

Ethan: feels normal now. just weird remembering how intense it was.

metadata.title: Thunder Grind chat – late night COVID talk

artifacts.content:
Workout / health log – looking back on COVID + soccer

Date: 2023-08-20

I’m starting 8th grade at Westview tomorrow and I want to remember how far I’ve come since getting COVID in March 2021.

Timeline recap:
– 3/24/21: first symptoms (tired, dry cough, low fever, lost taste/smell).
– 3/26/21: positive PCR at Riverside Community Health Clinic in Austin after the team exposure. Dad started the Google Doc that day, logging my temps and pulse oximeter readings (97–99% every time). He kept emailing updates to Dr. Chen and my homeroom teacher so they knew why I wasn’t in Zoom.
– Late March–early April: quarantined in my room. Mom rearranged the whole house so I stayed isolated. Sofia slept in the living room on a mattress for about two weeks. Grandma Rosa stopped going to her clients’ houses for those two weeks so she wouldn’t risk bringing anything back to us.
– 4/5/21: telehealth with Dr. Laura Chen at Barton Creek Pediatrics. She documented that I had no chest pain, palpitations, or fainting, and gave me a 7–10 day gradual return-to-play plan for soccer. Dad told her again about Grandpa Miguel’s heart problems in Phoenix and his long COVID worries; she said it wasn’t directly related to my case.
– Early May 2021: fully back to sprinting and scrimmages, no breathing problems.
– 6/18/21 & 7/9/21: Pfizer doses at Austin Community Vaccination Center.
– 1/21/22: Pfizer booster there; Mom told the nurse about Tío Luis’s week-long COVID hospitalization in San Antonio last summer.

Now: training 3–4 nights a week with Tucson Thunder at Arthur Pack, plus runs with Dad on the path near 4827 W Calle Loma. No issues. Good to remember this when I get nervous before tournaments.

Notes to self:

- Has Your Child Fainted Or Passed Out During Or After Exercise Emotion Or Startle: false
- Has Your Child Ever Had Extreme Shortness Of Breath During Exercise: false
- Has Your Child Had Extreme Fatigue Associated With Exercise Different From Other Children: false
- Has Your Child Ever Had Discomfort Pain Or Pressure In His Her Chest During Exercise: false
- Has Your Child Ever Been Diagnosed With An Unexplained Seizure Disorder: false
- Has Your Child Ever Been Diagnosed With Exercise Induced Asthma Not Well Controlled With Medication: false
- Has Your Child Been Diagnosed With Multi Inflammatory Syndrome In Children Mis C: false
